title: S3LLM: Large-Scale Scientific Software Understanding with LLMs using Source, Metadata, and Document
abstract: Demonstrates the potential of using locally deployed open-source LLMs for the rapid understanding of large-scale scientific computing software, eliminating the need for extensive coding expertise, and thereby making the process more efficient and effective.
methodology: Using locally deployed open-source LLMs for understanding large-scale scientific computing software

problem_addressed.description: Understanding large-scale scientific software is challenging due to multiple programming languages, large code volume, and inadequate documentation
specific_challenges: Multiple programming languages including older ones like Fortran and Pascal
Large volume of code (millions of lines)

existing_solutions.name: Doxygen
existing_solutions.purpose: Documentation generation and static code analysis

existing_solutions.name: Sphinx
existing_solutions.purpose: Documentation across various formats (HTML, LaTeX, ePub, Texinfo, manual pages, plain text)

limitations_of_existing_solutions: Primarily tailored for static code analysis
Lack capability to accommodate dynamic queries
Challenge in formulating queries in both instructed and structured formats

proposed_solution.name: S3LLM
proposed_solution.description: A framework providing a user-friendly interface for interacting with complex scientific computing software through conversational, natural language queries
proposed_solution.approach: Using large language models to make software comprehension more accessible and intuitive
